ctrl+arrowRight/left can be used from audEp entered state to cycle selection target on audSegs (same style as audEP cycle selection);
enter press while targeting audSeg  will lock to that target and clamp audio playback to its start and end times (playback wraps), also seeking to its start time

audSeg items will reorganize as follows when an enter press locks an audSeg to entered state: 
1) IF the entered audSeg is not the first audSeg, it will be moved to occupy its own row alone. its width will become the entire row. audSegs after it will be moved to continue as normal on following row(s). If it is the first audSeg, it will fill the current row and others will be moved down. If it is the last audSeg, it will be moved down to its own row and will occupy the whole row. when entered state exited, the default 3-cards per row will be restored.
2)
